purpose: Conformance fixture for GuardrailProvider / revise_args pattern. Demonstrates that action_ref is the correlation key across the three-record trail (pre-execution, decision, receipt) and is derived from the intent tuple — not from the args. original_args_digest and effective_args_digest are separate fields in the pre-execution record, enabling a verifier to reconstruct whether and how args were revised without trusting the agent runtime. authorization_ref binds both records to the specific authorization decision — a verifier can confirm not just same call and same dispatched payload, but same approval. Three verifier states: AUTHORIZED_EXECUTION (matching decision_binding_ref, unexpired, effective_args_digest == original_args_digest under same live decision), OBSERVABLE_NOT_AUTHORIZED (well-formed receipt, no matching decision_binding_ref), QUIET_SWAP_OR_STALE (decision exists but effective args, expiry, policy_id, or decision_ts no longer match).
reproduce_in_python:
import hashlib, json
def jcs(obj): return json.dumps(obj, separators=(',',':'), sort_keys=True, ensure_ascii=False)
action_ref = hashlib.sha256(jcs(preimage).encode()).hexdigest()
args_digest = hashlib.sha256(jcs(args).encode()).hexdigest()
authorization_ref = hashlib.sha256(jcs(auth_preimage).encode()).hexdigest()

scenario.description: An agent requests a database query tool call. The GuardrailProvider intercepts, inspects the original args (limit=1000), revises them (limit=100), and emits a pre-execution record before allowing the call to proceed. The receipt is emitted post-execution. A verifier correlates pre-execution record and receipt via action_ref — recomputable from the intent tuple alone — and confirms both records reference the same authorization decision via authorization_ref.

positive_path.description: Full arc: intent tuple → action_ref → authorization decision → pre-execution record (with revision) → receipt. action_ref and authorization_ref are stable across all records.

step_1_action_ref.label: Derive action_ref from intent tuple (correlation key)

step_2_args_digests.label: Compute separate digests for original and effective args
step_2_args_digests.invariant: original_args_digest != effective_args_digest when guardrail revised the args

step_2b_authorization_ref.label: Derive authorization_ref from the authorization decision record
step_2b_authorization_ref.description: authorization_ref identifies the specific authorization decision — the policy snapshot and scope that were in force at the moment of approval. Recomputable by any verifier holding the decision record fields; no operator trust required.
step_2b_authorization_ref.invariant: action_ref is embedded in the authorization_ref preimage — a verifier can confirm the decision was for this specific action instance

step_3_pre_execution_record.label: Pre-execution record — emitted before tool call executes

step_4_receipt.label: Receipt — emitted post-execution, same action_ref and authorization_ref
step_4_receipt.verifier_check: action_ref and authorization_ref in receipt == action_ref and authorization_ref in pre_execution_record — same call instance, same dispatched payload, same authorization decision

negative_cases.label: action_ref derived from args instead of intent tuple
negative_cases.description: A non-conformant implementation computes action_ref = SHA-256(JCS(original_args)) instead of SHA-256(JCS({agent_id, action_type, scope, timestamp})). The resulting hash differs from the conformant action_ref. A verifier who recomputes from the intent tuple will detect the mismatch and cannot correlate the record.
negative_cases.verifier_outcome: REJECT — action_ref is not recomputable from intent tuple; implementation is non-conformant

negative_cases.label: original_args_digest masked to equal effective_args_digest
negative_cases.description: A non-conformant implementation sets original_args_digest = effective_args_digest, hiding the fact that the guardrail revised the args. A verifier checking the pre-execution record sees revised=true but cannot verify the extent of the revision — the args delta is unauditable.
negative_cases.invariant_violated: original_args_digest == effective_args_digest when revised=true — revision is unauditable
negative_cases.verifier_outcome: REJECT — a verifier cannot confirm args were actually revised; the record is inconsistent

negative_cases.label: Receipt carries a different action_ref — broken correlation
negative_cases.description: A non-conformant implementation emits a receipt with an action_ref derived from a slightly different intent tuple (timestamp 1 second off). The pre-execution record and receipt cannot be correlated by a verifier holding only the two records — the binding key is broken.
negative_cases.invariant_violated: pre_execution.action_ref != receipt.action_ref — three-record arc is broken
negative_cases.verifier_outcome: REJECT — verifier cannot correlate pre-execution record to receipt; records belong to different action instances

negative_cases.label: Stripe financial fields changed after approval — gate rejects before call
negative_cases.description: Agent proposes a Stripe charge for amount=9900 USD. Approval is granted; authorization_ref binds to original_args_digest over the approved fields. Before the Stripe API call, the executor presents effective args with amount=14900 — idempotency_key and customer unchanged. The gate detects effective_args_digest != original_args_digest with no authorized guardrail revision in between. A conformant gate MUST reject before dispatching to Stripe. The approval for args D does not carry forward to args D'.
negative_cases.scenario: Stripe payment agent — charge revision without new approval
negative_cases.invariant_violated: attempted_effective_args_digest != original_args_digest with no authorized guardrail revision — old approval does not carry forward
negative_cases.verifier_outcome: REJECT — gate must deny execution before Stripe call; a new approval record for effective_args_digest is required
negative_cases.note: idempotency_key is intentionally unchanged to confirm the gate checks the full args digest, not just the idempotency key

independent_reproductions.method: JCS (RFC 8785) canonicalization + SHA-256, computed with an independent implementation that shares no code with this fixture's generator
independent_reproductions.result: All four NEG-5 digests reproduce byte-identical from the stated preimages. Two independent stacks now sit on the deny condition: attempted_effective_args_digest does not equal original_args_digest with no authorized revision in between, so a conformant gate rejects before the Stripe call. The prior approval over original_args_digest does not carry forward to the changed args.

negative_cases.label: Receipt carries a different authorization_ref — execution under a different approval
negative_cases.description: effective_args_digest is unchanged (same dispatched payload), but the receipt carries an authorization_ref derived from a different decision window (decision_ts 60 seconds earlier, different policy snapshot). A verifier can prove 'same call and same dispatched payload' but cannot confirm 'same authorization decision' — the execution may have proceeded under a stale or different approval.
negative_cases.invariant_violated: pre_execution.authorization_ref != receipt.authorization_ref — execution was tied to a different authorization decision
negative_cases.verifier_outcome: REJECT — verifier can confirm same call and same dispatched payload, but cannot confirm same authorization decision; the approval binding is broken

negative_cases.label: Missing decision_binding_ref — receipt is observable but not authorized
negative_cases.description: A well-formed receipt carries a valid action_ref recomputable from the intent tuple, but no decision_binding_ref field. The receipt proves the action was observed; it does not prove the action was authorized. A verifier that requires decision_binding_ref to classify AUTHORIZED_EXECUTION must classify this as OBSERVABLE_NOT_AUTHORIZED. Absence of the field is the condition — not a format error.
negative_cases.positive_control: receipt is format-valid; action_ref recomputes correctly from intent tuple — this is not a malformed record
negative_cases.note: action_ref is valid and recomputable; decision_binding_ref is absent — not malformed, just unbound to any authorization decision
negative_cases.invariant_violated: receipt.decision_binding_ref is absent — no authorization decision is bound to this execution
negative_cases.verifier_outcome: OBSERVABLE_NOT_AUTHORIZED — receipt is well-formed and action_ref is valid, but the execution cannot be classified as authorized without a matching decision_binding_ref

negative_cases.label: decision_binding_ref resolves to a block/review decision — receipt cannot claim authorized execution
negative_cases.description: The pre-execution decision record carries outcome: 'block' (or 'review'). A terminal receipt is later presented with a decision_binding_ref that resolves back to that block decision. A verifier holding the decision record can confirm the referenced decision did not authorize execution — outcome 'block' means the action was explicitly denied. The receipt cannot be used as proof of authorized execution regardless of whether decision_binding_ref is formally valid.
negative_cases.positive_control: receipt is format-valid and decision_binding_ref resolves to a real decision record — this is not a malformed receipt
negative_cases.note: decision_binding_ref in receipt resolves to a decision with outcome='block'. The binding is structurally valid but semantically denying. A verifier must inspect the referenced decision outcome, not just confirm the ref resolves.
negative_cases.invariant_violated: pre_execution_decision_record.outcome == 'block' — decision_binding_ref resolves to a denial, not an authorization; receipt cannot claim AUTHORIZED_EXECUTION
negative_cases.verifier_outcome: QUIET_SWAP_OR_STALE — decision exists and decision_binding_ref resolves, but the referenced decision did not authorize execution; presenting this receipt as proof of authorization is a quiet swap

negative_cases.label: decision_binding_ref resolves to a different action_ref — cross-action binding
negative_cases.description: A receipt presents a decision_binding_ref that is structurally valid and resolves to a real authorization decision, but that decision was issued for a different action_ref (different intent tuple). A verifier recomputing decision_binding_ref from the referenced decision's preimage will find action_ref_in_decision != receipt.action_ref. The receipt cannot claim authorization for this action instance — the approval was for a different call.
negative_cases.note: decision_binding_ref resolves correctly to the NEG-5 Stripe decision (action_ref e6dd416e…). The receipt action_ref is 104812928e… (autogen:guardrail intent). The two action_refs do not match — this decision was not issued for this action.
negative_cases.positive_control: decision_binding_ref is structurally valid and resolves to a real decision record — this is not a malformed ref
negative_cases.invariant_violated: decision.action_ref (e6dd416e…) != receipt.action_ref (104812928e…) — the referenced authorization was issued for a different action instance
negative_cases.verifier_outcome: QUIET_SWAP_OR_STALE — decision_binding_ref resolves but the bound decision was issued for a different action_ref; this receipt cannot claim authorization for the current action instance
